disable_gatekeeper_modal_privacy_text.message: 如果您將 URL 添加到此軟體以用於抓取 JSON 檔案和雜湊檔案，則該擴充功能會連接到您指定的 URL，以便每次啟用並在您的瀏覽器中載入該擴充功能時，從遠端抓取規則 JSON 檔案或雜湊檔案。 每次它根據您定義的 URL 抓取檔案時，託管這些檔案（例如 JSON 和雜湊檔案）的伺服器也會接收到您的 IP 位址以及一般資訊，例如您的擴充功能版本、瀏覽器和瀏覽器版本。 此資料受該網站或網路伺服器（其中託管這些檔案）的隱私權政策約束。

audit_faq_modal_title.description: Title for the audit FAQ modal

audit_faq_intro.message: These notes describe the provider fields that clearurls.js actually reads when it builds and applies rules.
audit_faq_intro.description: Intro text for the audit FAQ modal

audit_faq_audit_provider_title.message: How do I audit a provider?
audit_faq_audit_provider_title.description: FAQ title for auditing a provider

audit_faq_audit_provider_body.message:
Open a source in the viewer, find the provider inside providers, and inspect the keys on that provider object.

Use Bundled Rules to inspect the shipped rules, Final Rules to inspect the merged runtime result, Custom Rules to inspect user overrides, and Latest Cached Remote Rules to inspect the most recently fetched remote payload saved in cache.
audit_faq_audit_provider_body.description: FAQ body for auditing a provider

audit_faq_override_title.message: How do I enable, disable, import, edit, or override individual rules?
audit_faq_override_title.description: FAQ title for custom rule editing and overrides

audit_faq_override_body.message:
Use the Custom Rules Editor when you want rule-by-rule control. That is the place where you can import rules as custom rules, edit provider fields, and save your own version of a provider.

You can use it to enable or disable individual providers or individual rule content by turning the imported or edited provider into your custom version. That custom version is then merged into the runtime rules.

You can also override bundled rules and remote rules individually. Audit Bundled Rules or Latest Cached Remote Rules to find the provider you want, then inspect Custom Rules and Final Rules to confirm your custom edit or override is taking effect.

If you import a ruleset into the Custom Rules Editor, you can keep only the parts you want, disable the parts you do not want, and edit the provider so the final merged output behaves the way you intend.
audit_faq_override_body.description: FAQ body for custom rule editing and overrides

audit_faq_url_pattern_body.message:
urlPattern is the regex used to decide whether a provider matches a URL.

If a provider has urlPattern, ClearURLs uses that regex first. In clearurls.js, this is turned into a real RegExp by setURLPattern().

audit_faq_domain_patterns_body.message:
domainPatterns is the domain-based matcher used when a provider does not rely on urlPattern.

ClearURLs stores these with setURLDomainPattern() and then checks them with domain matching logic instead of regex URL matching.

audit_faq_rules_title.message: What are rules, rawRules, and referralMarketing?
audit_faq_rules_title.description: FAQ title for rules, rawRules, and referralMarketing

audit_faq_rules_body.message:
rules are parameter names or fragment names that should be removed from the URL.

rawRules are regex replacements applied directly to the whole URL string before query cleanup.

referralMarketing is treated like removable parameters too, but whether they are actually used depends on the global referral-marketing setting.
audit_faq_rules_body.description: FAQ body for rules, rawRules, and referralMarketing

audit_faq_exceptions_title.description: FAQ title for exceptions and domainExceptions

audit_faq_exceptions_body.message:
exceptions are regex patterns that stop a provider from acting on matching URLs.

domainExceptions do the same thing with domain-based matching instead of URL regex matching.

If an exception matches, the provider is bypassed even if its main pattern matched first.
audit_faq_exceptions_body.description: FAQ body for exceptions and domainExceptions

audit_faq_redirections_title.description: FAQ title for redirections and domainRedirections

audit_faq_redirections_body.message:
redirections are regex-based unwrappers. They usually capture the real destination URL in group 1, and ClearURLs redirects to that captured value.

domainRedirections are domain-style redirect rules. In this codebase they are checked for entries shaped like pattern $redirect= target.

If a regex redirection matches first, that wins. If not, ClearURLs checks domain redirections next.
audit_faq_redirections_body.description: FAQ body for redirections and domainRedirections

audit_faq_methods_resources_title.description: FAQ title for methods and resourceTypes

audit_faq_methods_resources_body.message:
methods restrict a provider to specific HTTP methods such as GET or POST. If no methods are listed, all methods are allowed.

resourceTypes restrict a provider to webRequest resource types such as main_frame, sub_frame, or xmlhttprequest.

If no resource types are listed, ClearURLs falls back to the global allowed request types from settings.
audit_faq_methods_resources_body.description: FAQ body for methods and resourceTypes

audit_faq_complete_force_title.description: FAQ title for completeProvider and forceRedirection

audit_faq_complete_force_body.message:
completeProvider turns the provider into a blocking provider. In clearurls.js, that means the provider can cancel matching requests when domain blocking is enabled.

forceRedirection makes a redirect more aggressive. When a redirection is found on a main_frame request, ClearURLs can update the browser tab directly instead of returning a normal redirect response.
audit_faq_complete_force_body.description: FAQ body for completeProvider and forceRedirection

audit_faq_fields_title.message: Which provider fields does clearurls.js use here?
audit_faq_fields_title.description: FAQ title for the list of provider fields used by clearurls.js

audit_faq_fields_body.message:
The provider-building path in this branch reads these keys: completeProvider, forceRedirection, urlPattern, domainPatterns, rules, rawRules, referralMarketing, exceptions, domainExceptions, redirections, domainRedirections, methods, and resourceTypes.

When auditing behavior, the most important place to check is Final Rules, because that shows the merged result after bundled, remote, and custom sources have been combined.
audit_faq_fields_body.description: FAQ body for the list of provider fields used by clearurls.js
